schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: # 4) Multiscale/Multifractal twist (useful for TZP) Make \(N\) vary with scale to get a **multifractal** (more realistic cosmological/TZP behavior). For example, choose a sequence \(N_k\) reflecting different effective kissing numbers at different scales (micro, meso, macro). Then the local dimension at a point depends on the asymptotic growth rate: \[ D_{\mathrm{local}} = \lim_{k\to\infty} \frac{\log(N_0 N_1\cdots N_{k-1})}{- \log (r^k)}. \] If the \(N_k\) cycle or follow a rule related to spherical harmonic modes (e.g., \(N_k\) increases near modes with triadic symmetry), the TZP becomes naturally inhomogeneous and scale-dependent — a richer model for “bifurication” behavior.
theory.technical_interpretation: Multiscale/Multifractal Twist (Useful for TZP): D_local is a limiting process, written D_local = lim_k→∞ fraclog(N_0 N_1·s N_k-1)- log (r^k).. It is defined through a limit, describing the behaviour of the quantity as a parameter approaches a critical value. It is built from k, N_0, N_1, N_k-1, r, defining D_local. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where then the local dimension at a point depends on the asymptotic growth rate
